title: Print Job Models
text: Print Job Models A print job model is a named collection of printer attributes that determine how and where Uniface forms or reports are printed, including details of the device type, margins, and so on. A print job model must be available before you can print. The default print job model is PRINTER, which is used if you don't specify another one, or if no other is available. The PRINTER model uses the P_DEFAULT device translation table, which strips all character attribute codes from the printout and converts special characters to basic ASCII. Uniface supports a large number of printers and provides default device translation tables for both ASCII and generic PostScript. The name of each print job model supplied with Uniface begins with P_ , for example, P_LN03 . Print job models are stored in the PRATT entity of the PRINTER application model in the Repository. Related Links Define a Print Job Model You must define a print job model before you can print data from Form components. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
